description: TypeScript service class with unbounded cache, SQL injection, null-deref on find, unguarded Promise.all, and stale closure in setInterval

issues.question: Q24
issues.description: Unbounded cache: private cache: Map<string, any> accumulates entries indefinitely — no eviction, TTL, or size cap; long-running processes will exhaust memory
issues.expected_fix: add TTL-based eviction (like logCache pattern in same class) or use LRU with a max size

issues.question: Q2
issues.description: SQL injection: template literal `SELECT * FROM items WHERE id=${id}` interpolates id directly into the query string — id comes from the caller and is unsanitized
issues.expected_fix: use parameterized query: this.db.query('SELECT * FROM items WHERE id = ?', [id])

issues.question: Q1
issues.description: Missing null check on Array.find result: result.name accessed without guard — Array.find returns undefined when no element matches, causing TypeError at runtime
issues.expected_fix: check result before access: if (!result) throw new Error(...) or return a default

issues.question: Q8
issues.description: No error boundary in Promise.all: one rejected item causes the entire batch to reject, losing all successful results — callers receive an error instead of partial results
issues.expected_fix: use Promise.allSettled or wrap each item: items.map(id => this.processItem(id).catch(err => ({ itemId: id, success: false, error: String(err) })))

issues.question: Q1
issues.description: Stale closure: const threshold = config.alertThreshold captured at construction time inside setInterval — later changes to config.alertThreshold are never seen by the interval callback
issues.expected_fix: reference this.config.alertThreshold directly inside the callback instead of capturing a local copy

false_positive_traps: Line ~404: this.db.query('SELECT * FROM logs WHERE user_id = ?', [userId]) — parameterized query, NOT SQL injection
Line ~246: logCache (Map with TTL eviction via expiresAt) — NOT unbounded; evictExpiredLogs() manages it
